video_title: Devil May Cry: Season 2 | First Look | Netflix
comment_text:
I don't remember whether Dante or any of the humans were aware of how that mimic/shape-shifter demon's ability worked, but I vaguely recall some character mentioning they can only imitate people they've seen, or came into contact with or something like that, but even that doesn't explain how that demon would know the kind of apparel Vergil would be wearing, style of clothing, all that jazz...so shouldn't it have been suspicious for Dante how a shapeshifter demon would know how exactly his (supposedly dead) twin would even dress? And they dared to use a rendition of Bury the Light, when it wasn't even the real Vergil, to begin with. Nice bait and switch! D - for Disrespectful! Aside from that, they made Dante criminally stupid. He was never stupid in the games. A little immature, goofy, cavalier (*snort*), but never stupid. Did they really think dumbing him down was charming? 

The new aspect I was looking forward to exploring the most was him actually interacting with human authorities and they kinda botched that, as well, particularly with how they tossed in bosses from 4 and 5, like Echidna, who no human should've been able to even touch, never mind defeat on their own, without some significant help, either from Dante or by a whole friggin' military. And I wouldn't be surprised if this whole "Makai" world they came up with out of the blue, with the refugee demons, was just a rather thinly veiled excuse/plot-device to make a reference to the immigration issue.

This sadly didn't deserve a second season. The old animated series was much MUCH better, in tone, and characterization, at least. The overarching plot wasn't that spectacular, but actually pretty standard DMC, on a smaller scale than what the games had. This series, they wanted to do too much, and did none of it with any compelling and meaningful depth or enough development.
